response: The singer who sang "Four Seasons of Love," Donna Summer, sold over 140 million records worldwide. She was an American singer, songwriter, and painter, gaining prominence during the disco era of the late 1970s. She was a five-time Grammy Award winner and achieved three consecutive double albums reaching No. 1 on the Billboard 200 chart. She also charted four number-one singles in the U.S. within a 12-month period and two number-one singles on the R&B charts in the U.S.
